title: Eranthemum acanthophorum
intro: Eranthemum acanthophorum, also known as the 'Nerium', is a plant species in the family Acanthaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in reducing fever, pain, and inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Eranthemum acanthophorum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in the form of a paste or a powder to treat various health conditions, including skin diseases and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Eranthemum acanthophorum is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It belongs to the genus Eranthemum and is native to tropical Asia. The plant is commonly known by several names, including blue throat, blue throat flower, and blue throat plant. Eranthemum acanthophorum is a shrub or undershrub that grows up to 1 meter in height and has blue to purple flowers. The plant is often used as an ornamental in tropical gardens due to its attractive flowers and compact growth habit.
